page: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dplsv6 server family. It is powered by a Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor with an arm64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The server features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs, utilizing a single thread per core configuration. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This configuration is designated as a low-memory profile. The virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core capabilities alongside moderate single-core performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and Geekbench multi-core score both rank in the top tier and strong categories, reflecting the efficiency of the 48 dedicated physical cores. Memory performance is a notable strength, with uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks placing in the top tier. Passmark database operations and physics tests also achieved top-tier results. However, single-core metrics, such as the stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core score, remain in the average category. Large language model inference benchmarks show a clear tradeoff, with prompt processing for a 70B model registering as weak, while text generation for a 7B model remains strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48pls_v6 offers a high density of dedicated physical ARM cores relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory arrays. The lack of bundled local storage requires reliance on external premium storage, which should be factored into architectural planning. This server is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, where its high memory bandwidth and multi-core performance can be fully leveraged.
description: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dplsv6 family, featuring 48 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, representing a low-memory profile of 2.0 GB per core, and does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the server delivers top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, as evidenced by strong Geekbench multi-core and Passmark database operation benchmarks. Conversely, single-core performance and large-model LLM prompt processing represent comparative tradeoffs, scoring in the average and weak ranges respectively. This server is suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 96 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 48 dedicated cores, 96 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and high memory bandwidth optimized for compute-intensive, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM64 Neoverse-N2 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core benchmark performance
No local storage included, with support for premium external storage
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving
